
  The pipeline's writer can't produce these. render() (parser.py:64-73) always emits status:
  plan.status.value, which is by construction a valid enum member. The only way a file gets
  an invalid status: is if something other than the pipeline wrote it. And it's not a case of
  "we renamed enum values and old files went stale" — PlanStatus has been exactly
  draft/accepted/superseded since the initial scaffold (2e8fbf3); open and active were never
  valid here.

  The two files confirm external authorship:
  - plan-jk-crash.md uses a foreign frontmatter schema entirely — title:, created:, reopened:
  instead of the canonical name:/created_at:/revisions:. This never round-tripped through
  render(); it was authored by hand or imported from elsewhere.
  - plan-tui-polish-cleanup.md is canonical-format (name:, created_at:, revisions: 3) but
  carries status: active and a supersedes: extra — i.e. a hand/agent edit of the status field
  after the fact (had it round-tripped, status would be a valid value).

  So: not a codebase-change artifact. But the current pipeline will re-raise parse_error any
  time a human or agent edits frontmatter to an unsupported status — reconcile_file scans
  every *.md and parses it (sync.py:189-201). So it's reachable today, just only via
  out-of-band edits; the pipeline never self-generates it. This one is arguably "working as
  intended" (refusing to ingest garbage).

  conflict → reachable today, and won't self-heal

  This is the live concern. The branch at sync.py:223-231 fires whenever both the DB body and
  the markdown diverged from last_materialized_hash (db_changed and file_changed). That's a
  plain two-writer race: a planning agent writes a new revision into the DB while the file is
  edited out-of-band (editor, agent, git checkout) — both since the last materialization.
  With a 1.5s poll / 0.75s debounce, that window is real and reachable in normal operation.

  Worse, it's sticky. mark_plan_sync_state updates only file_hash — not
  last_materialized_hash or body_hash (plans.py:251-259). So once conflicted, db_changed and
  file_changed both stay True on every subsequent poll and it re-evaluates to conflict
  indefinitely. Editing the file again doesn't help (keeps file_changed true), and the
  DB-wins path (materialize_row at sync.py:232-234) only runs when db_changed and not
  file_changed — unreachable while the file still diverges. I checked the service layer:
  there's no "resolve conflict" affordance — read_model.py:329 just displays the conflict
  banner, and open_plan_in_editor just re-runs reconcile_name, which re-lands on conflict.
  The only code paths that force sync_state='synced' are rename_plan and deprecate_plan
  (sync.py:109, plans.py:223).

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
❯ 
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle) · esc to interrupt
